primary_factors: No winter precip expected (0" snow; no freezing rain signal) and temperatures remain above freezing during bus hours (~39–40°F)
No extreme cold/wind chill risk (feels-like ~32°F); extreme-cold closure rules not triggered
Road agencies/infrastructure posture indicates roads are already clear; no plow catch-up scenario needed
Main operational risks are *non-snow*: patchy fog/low stratus and localized lingering low-spot flooding near the Grand River corridor
No confirmed neighboring-district closures; no Rockford-specific closure messaging found
decision_rationale:
All core experts (meteorology, safety, historian) agree this is not a winter-weather closure setup for Rockford: temperatures stay above freezing through the commute window, no accumulating snow is forecast, and no credible source indicates dangerous wind chills. The one apparent conflict—local news noting the Kent County Road Commission preparing for “anticipated icy roads”—was clarified: that FOX17 item was focused on a *midweek* (Wednesday night into Thursday) icing window, not Thu night into Fri (3/20) morning, so it should not be overweighted for Friday. Plow-timing math is effectively moot because there is no snow event to clear; infrastructure assessment expects roads to be passable with only fog as a meaningful travel limiter.

Residual closure risk is kept non-zero mainly for non-snow operational disruptions: (1) patchy fog briefly reducing visibility on rural/low-lying routes (meteorologist notes potential minima near ~1 mile, but winds/mixing toward daybreak should limit widespread dense fog), (2) localized lingering flood/standing-water issues near river-adjacent low spots, and (3) a localized power/outage-at-a-building scenario. Power conditions: there is an elevated but not extreme ongoing outage count region-wide; however, no Kent/Rockford-specific clustering or facility impacts are confirmed in the accessible outage views, and Friday’s benign weather is not conducive to *new* outages. With no neighboring-district closure domino signals, the most Brier-optimal call is a very low probability and “Open with Monitoring” rather than hedging upward.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 0.0% spread. Average probability: 2%.
